app_summary: RedReader is an Android application that allows users to access and interact with content from Reddit using the Reddit API. It provides a user-friendly interface for browsing subreddits, viewing posts, and managing user preferences. The app also includes settings for customizing the user experience, such as adjusting font sizes and selecting themes.

core_workflows.description: This workflow represents the user's journey through the app's main screens. Starting from the user agreement screen (0), the user navigates to the home screen (1), then to the subreddit selection screen (2), and subsequently to the login prompt (3). After logging in, the user is taken to the authorization error screen (4) and then to the web authentication screen (5). The user then returns to the home screen (2) and finally back to the user agreement screen (0).
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow is important for users to understand and accept the app's terms and conditions before using its core features.

core_workflows.description: This workflow begins with the user agreement screen (0) and follows a path through various subreddit selection and management screens. The user navigates from the home screen (1) to the subreddit selection screen (2), then to the user agreement screen (6), and subsequently to the subreddit selection screen (7). After selecting a subreddit, the user is taken to the subreddit management screen (8), then to the front page screen (9), and finally to the themes screen (10). The user then navigates through the font settings screens (11 and 12) and returns to the front page screen (10), then to the subreddit management screen (9), and finally to the subreddit selection screen (13). The user then navigates through the user agreement screen (14) and returns to the subreddit selection screen (13), and finally to the subreddit management screen (9).
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow is important for users to manage their subreddits, customize their app experience, and access the front page of Reddit.

core_workflows.description: This workflow is similar to the previous one but includes additional steps for managing pinned subreddits and accessing the main settings menu. After navigating through the subreddit management screen (9), the user is taken to the subreddit selection screen (15), then to the subreddit management screen (16), and subsequently to the user agreement screen (7). The user then navigates through the subreddit selection screen (8), the subreddit management screen (9), and the front page screen (10). The user then navigates through the themes screen (11), the font settings screens (12 and 10), and the subreddit management screen (9). The user then navigates to the pinned subreddit management screen (17), then to the subreddit selection screen (18), and subsequently to the main settings menu (19). The user then navigates through the font settings screens (20 to 25) and the main settings menu (26), and finally to the main settings menu (27).
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow is important for users to manage their pinned subreddits, access the main settings menu, and customize their app experience.

screen_contexts.context: The screen displays a user agreement for RedReader, an application that accesses content using the Reddit API. It asks the user to read and accept the terms and conditions by clicking on the provided link.
The screen displays a Settings menu with various options: Appearance (Font, Behaviour, Images/Video, Cache, Network, Menus, Accessibility, Backup/Restore), About.

The screen displays a home screen for the RedReader application. It shows the current time (11:30), the app name (RedReader), and three main sections: Front Page, All Subreddits, and Find Location with a search icon. Below these, it lists some subscribed subreddits such as 'art', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', 'documentaries', 'earthporn', and others. There is a 'Log in' button at the bottom, indicating that the user is currently not logged in.

The screen displays the main RedReader app interface with the following sections: 'Front Page', 'All Subreddits', 'Find Location' search bar, 'Subscribed Subreddits', and a list of subscribed subreddits including 'ar', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', 'documentaries', 'earthporn', 'creepy'. The screen also shows the current time as '11:31'.

The screen displays a list of 'Pinned Subreddits' with the currently selected subreddit '/r/diy' highlighted. Below this, it shows a list of 'Subscribed Subreddits' including 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'. The screen also shows the current time as '11:31'.

The screen displays a message indicating that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted. It provides two options: 'DETAILS' and 'VIEW AGREEMENT'. The screen also shows the current time as '11:32' and the currently selected subreddit as '/r/aww'.
The screen displays a login prompt for Reddit. It lists some subscribed subreddits like 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', etc. There's a disclaimer about the login process and a link for help.
The screen shows a list of subreddits including 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', etc.
The screen displays a list of pinned subreddits like 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', etc. It provides options to 'Copy Link', 'View in External Browser', 'Share', 'Block Subreddit'.

The screen displays an error message indicating that the web page at 'https://www.reddit.com/api/v1/authorize.compact?...' could not be loaded due to a network disconnect. The screen also shows the time '11:31'.
The screen displays a message stating that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted. It shows the current subreddit '/r/documentaries' and the time '11:32'.

The screen displays the main home screen of the RedReader app. It shows various sections such as 'Front Page', 'All Subreddits', 'Pinned Subreddits', and 'Subscribed Subreddits'. The app logo and the time '11:32' are also visible. A list of subscribed subreddits is present, including 'diY', 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', and 'creepy'.
The screen displays an error message indicating that the web page at 'https://www.reddit.com/api/v1/authorize.compact?...' could not be loaded due to a network disconnect.

The screen displays the main home screen of the RedReader application. It shows the front page, all subreddits, and pinned subreddits. There are options to copy links, view in an external browser, share, block, and pin to main menu.

The screen displays a list of subscribed subreddits within the RedReader application. It shows the front page, all subreddits, and pinned subreddits. The user is currently viewing the 'diy' subreddit. Other subscribed subreddits listed include 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.
The screen displays a message indicating that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted. To access content through the Reddit API, the user is prompted to accept the agreement.

The screen displays a list of subscribed subreddits within the RedReader application. It shows the front page, all subreddits, and pinned subreddits. The user is currently viewing the 'diy' subreddit. Other subscribed subreddits listed include 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.
The screen displays a warning message indicating that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted. It also provides a link to view the agreement details.
The screen displays the front page of Reddit with the 'hot' feed. It shows a user's name ('agreemen') and a 'VIEW AGREEMENT' button.
The screen displays a theme selection menu with color options: Night, Night (low contrast), Green, Blue, Light Blue, Orange, Gray, and Ultra Black.

The screen displays font settings with options to adjust the font scale globally and for specific elements like post titles, subtitles, comment headers, and body text. The screen title is 'Font Scale' and the current setting is 'Normal'.
The screen displays a font scale adjustment menu within the settings. It shows various font sizes ranging from 0.7x to 1.7x with 'Cancel' as the only button at the bottom.

The screen displays a settings menu for font scaling. It offers options to adjust the font scale for post titles, subtitles, and comment headers. The global font scale can be used for most elements, while comment headers are set to 0.7x.
The screen displays a font scale adjustment menu for subtitles. It offers options ranging from 0.7x to 1.7x, with 'Normal' as the default. The 'Cancel' button is present at the bottom.
The screen displays a settings menu for font customization. It includes options for Font Scale, Post titles, Post subtitles, Comment/Inbox headers, Body text, and Link buttons.
The screen displays a settings menu for adjusting the global font scale. It offers options ranging from 0.7x to 1.7x, with 'Normal' as the default. The 'Cancel' button is present at the bottom.

The screen displays a settings menu for font sizes. It includes options for global font scale, post titles, post subtitles, opened post titles, opened post subtitles, comment/inbox headers, body text, and link buttons. The time '11:33 243' is displayed at the top.
The screen displays the main settings menu with various categories: Appearance, Font, Behaviour, Images/Video, Cache, Network, Menus, Accessibility, Backup/Restore, and About.
